Startup Guide

Low MOQ Clothing Manufacturer: How to Start Your Brand From Just 50 Pieces

✎ FABRIOZA Team 📅 July 1, 2026 ☕ 11 min read
Home / Blog / Low MOQ Clothing Manufacturer: How to Start Your Brand From Just 50 Pieces

Most clothing factories require 300-1,000 units minimum per style. For a startup brand testing its first collection, that means investing $5,000-$15,000 before making a single sale. At FABRIOZA, we fundamentally changed this equation — offering professional low MOQ clothing manufacturing starting at just 50 pieces.

What Does MOQ Mean and Why Does It Kill Most Startup Clothing Brands?

MOQ stands for Minimum Order Quantity — the smallest number of units a factory will produce in a single order. For startup clothing brands, high MOQ is the single biggest barrier to entry.

Consider the math: a typical Chinese factory requires 500 hoodie minimums at $14 per unit. That's $7,000 invested before you know if the product will sell. Research shows that up to 90% of fashion startups fail, often because high MOQ forces overinvestment before the brand has validated demand.

The result? Brands either take on massive inventory risk or settle for low-quality uncertified factories that accept small orders but deliver inconsistent products.

Why Does FABRIOZA Achieve 50pcs MOQ When Others Require 300+?

The answer lies in Sialkot's unique manufacturing ecosystem. Unlike factories in China that import fabric from distant provinces in 300-500 meter minimum rolls, FABRIOZA sources fabric from mills located within 50 kilometers of our facility.

This local supply chain means:

  • Fabric is cut to your exact requirement — no surplus charged to you
  • No import duties on raw materials within Pakistan
  • 3-7 day fabric lead times vs 10-21 days from China
  • Lower inventory holding costs — we don't need to justify large fabric purchases

Combined with Pakistan's competitive labor costs and our ISO 9001 efficient production systems, we can profitably manufacture 50-piece orders without compromising quality.

The Real Risk Calculation — High MOQ vs Low MOQ

Let's compare two scenarios for a brand launching its first hoodie:

Factor500 Pieces (High MOQ)50 Pieces (FABRIOZA)
Upfront Investment$7,000$900
Storage Cost$100-300/monthMinimal
Unsold Inventory RiskHighLow
Design Iteration CostExpensiveAffordable
Cash Flow ImpactSevereMinimal

While the per-unit cost is slightly higher at 50 pieces, your total risk is 87% lower. That risk reduction is what saves startups and enables them to iterate based on real market feedback.

What You Can Order From FABRIOZA at 50 Pieces

Our 50-piece MOQ applies across our entire product range:

  • Hoodies and Sweatshirts: All GSM weights, all fabrics, all customization methods
  • Sportswear: Jerseys, training wear, gym sets
  • Team Uniforms: Soccer, basketball, cricket, esports
  • Streetwear: Oversized tees, joggers, cargo pants
  • Sublimation Apparel: All-over print jerseys and activewear
  • Private Label: Fully branded with labels, hangtags, packaging

All customization options are available at 50 pieces: custom woven labels, hangtags, embroidery, screen printing, sublimation, and puff print.

Low MOQ vs Low Quality — Why These Are Not the Same

A common misconception is that low MOQ equals low quality. At FABRIOZA, this couldn't be further from the truth.

Our ISO 9001:2015 certification requires the same quality management system to apply to every order regardless of size. The same production lines, the same QC inspectors, the same documented processes — whether you order 50 pieces or 5,000. The certification doesn't change based on order size, and neither does our commitment to quality.

How to Place Your First Low MOQ Order With FABRIOZA

  1. Send Inquiry: Product type, quantity, design reference or description
  2. Receive Quote: Within 24 hours with detailed pricing breakdown
  3. Sample Production: 7-10 days for your physical sample
  4. Review Sample: Check fit, fabric, construction, labels
  5. Approve Final Sample: Or request revisions (2 rounds included)
  6. Bulk Production: 3-4 weeks after sample approval
  7. Shipping: Air (5-7 days) or sea (25-35 days) with DDP available

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I mix different styles in one 50-piece order?

No, the 50-piece minimum applies per style. However, you can mix sizes and colors within that 50-piece minimum for a single style.

Does the 50pcs minimum apply per color or per style?

The 50-piece MOQ is per style, not per color. You can split your 50 pieces across 2-3 colors (minimum 15-20 pieces per color).

Can I order samples before the 50-piece bulk order?

Yes, we encourage it. Sample production is a required step before bulk. Sample costs $50-150 and is credited against your bulk order.

Is the unit price much higher at 50 pieces vs 200 pieces?

Typically 15-25% higher at 50 pieces compared to 200 pieces. However, your total cash outlay is much lower, reducing inventory risk significantly.

How do I get started if I don't have a tech pack?

No problem. Send us design references, sketches, or photos. Our design team creates production specifications from your references.
